evals.prompt: My elderly parents (both 70+) want a relaxed cultural trip to Kyoto in November 2026, mid-range comfort hotel, public transit. Plan a 5-day itinerary.
evals.expected_output: A day-by-day itinerary that respects a 4h/day activity cap for elderly travelers, prioritizes step-free routes, ends dinners by 18:00, surfaces the script's contradiction warnings (elderly + ambitious = no, elderly + late-nights = no), and includes a Skip-the-Trap section.
expectations: Output names an explicit 5-dim preference snapshot (Companion, Style, Pace, Accommodation, Rhythm)
Output applies elderly-specific lodging rules (ground-floor / lift-access / bathtub)
Output enforces a 4h/day activity cap for elderly companions (no ambitious schedule)
Output applies elderly-specific transit rules (step-free, private car or taxi preferred)
Output includes a Skip-the-Trap section with at least one city
Output includes daylight footer with sunset time for Kyoto in November

evals.prompt: Plan a 7-day road trip around Hokkaido in February 2026 for a group of 5 friends. We want a classic must-see Hokkaido experience, ambitious pace, premium hotel, late-night izakaya vibe.
evals.expected_output: A 7-day Hokkaido itinerary that respects ambitious + late-nights rhythm, applies the 30% transit buffer for self-drive, includes daylight cutoffs that account for February's 16:05 sunset, and routes geographically to avoid backtracking.
expectations: Output names the 5-dim snapshot including friends + ambitious + late-nights
Output applies 30% transit buffer to GPS estimates (self-drive)
Output routes geographically — base moves Hokkaido-east to Hokkaido-west or similar, no backtracking
Output includes daylight footer for at least one day (sunset 16:05 in Hokkaido Feb)
Output flags any Heavy Transit Day explicitly

evals.prompt: We're a family of 4 (2 adults + 2 kids ages 6 and 9) planning a relaxed nature trip to Bali in July 2026. Comfort hotel, self-drive, early starts. Plan a 6-day trip.
evals.expected_output: A 6-day Bali itinerary that applies the One Big Event rule (1 anchor/day for family), early-starts rhythm, nature focus (rice terraces, beaches, monkey forest), with hyper-local dish recommendations per meal.
expectations: Output names the 5-dim snapshot including family + nature + relaxed + early-starts
Output applies the One Big Event rule (max 1 anchor per day)
Output includes hyper-local dish recommendations (named dish + named venue) at least 3 times
Output ends with etiquette and packing sections
